requirement.original: Investigate which hook blocks /tmp/xlsx-venv/bin/python /root/orchestra/docs/dev/find_red_cells.py with stdout redirected to /root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3 despite a valid /allow sentinel grant; fix the gaps so this type of command is correctly honored.
requirement.clarified: Two hooks independently block the command. PRIMARY: pretool-tool-policy.py (matcher '*' fires for ALL tools) treats Bash redirect targets as Write targets; ba role lacks /root/orchestra/docs/research/ in allowed_write_path_prefixes → exits 2 (BLOCKED). SECONDARY: pretool-bash-safety.sh:483 sentinel block is guarded by IS_SUBAGENT != 1 — for subagent calls, the sentinel is never consulted. TERTIARY: task ID mismatch possible when CLAUDE_TASK_ID unset. Prior spec Gap C (redirect tokens in args_contain) was WRONG for bare-token /allow syntax — extra tokens become COMMENT, not args_contain. Prior R4 (allowlist.py /tmp path bug) was WRONG — string equality works correctly for /tmp paths.
requirement.what: Fix three gaps: (M1) add narrow write-path prefix (*/docs/research/case-) to ba.allowed_write_path_prefixes in policies/tool-policy.v1.json; (M2) extend sentinel grant check to IS_SUBAGENT=1 in pretool-bash-safety.sh; (M3) align task ID derivation between writer and reader
requirement.why: User needs to run Python scripts from venv interpreters installed under /tmp with output redirected to /root/orchestra/docs/research/. pretool-tool-policy.py enforces role-based write policy that blocks the redirect target for all non-dev roles, independent of any /allow grant.

included: policies/tool-policy.v1.json: add /root/orchestra/docs/research/ to ba and general-purpose allowed_write_path_prefixes (M1)
hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh: extend sentinel block to IS_SUBAGENT=1 for user-created grants; align task ID reader (M2, M3)
hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh: skip sentinel write for is_regex=true grants (S1)
excluded: IS_SUBAGENT firewall for legacy pattern-string grants (hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:145)
Subagent write firewall in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh Step 0
allowlist.py match logic — works correctly, no changes needed

success_criteria: pretool-tool-policy.py no longer exits 2 for ba subagent executing Bash command with redirect to /root/orchestra/docs/research/
pretool-bash-safety.sh sentinel block is reached and returns SENTINEL_OK for IS_SUBAGENT=1 when valid sentinel exists
Task ID derived by writer matches task ID used by reader for all four derivation scenarios
All daemon-restart, CF-1, and IS_SUBAGENT legacy firewalls remain fully intact
constraints: IS_SUBAGENT firewall for legacy grants at pretool-bash-safety.sh:145 must remain unchanged
CF-1 comment-only attack invariant at lines 517-526 must remain intact
Subagent write firewall in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh Step 0 must remain intact
All Layer 1.A-1.E daemon-restart prohibition blocks must remain intact
dev role allowed_write_path_prefixes must not be changed (already has *)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: _looks_regex() returns True for any pattern containing '.' (regex metacharacter). If a user uses a full-command /allow syntax where the script path with dots becomes the PATTERN (not COMMENT), the sentinel would be written with op='*' (regex path), triggering CF-1. This is path-external to the bare-token syntax that was actually used, but is a latent defect for users who use full-command /allow syntax.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: User issues /allow for /tmp venv Python command with output redirect to /root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3; command is still blocked despite valid sentinel grant
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: PRIMARY: pretool-tool-policy.py (hooks/pretool-tool-policy.py:47-92) fires for ALL tools (matcher '*') and treats Bash redirect targets as Write targets. is_allowed(role='ba', 'Write', '/root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3') fails because ba.allowed_write_path_prefixes in policies/tool-policy.v1.json does not include /root/orchestra/. SECONDARY: pretool-bash-safety.sh:483 sentinel block is guarded by IS_SUBAGENT != 1 — for subagent calls, the sentinel grant is never consulted regardless. TERTIARY: task ID mismatch potential when CLAUDE_TASK_ID unset and PreToolUse payload task_id field differs from session_id.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — this is a policy configuration gap, not a code regression. The tool-policy.v1.json ba role write prefixes never included /root/orchestra/.
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: pretool-tool-policy.py was designed as a role-based write fence. The ba role's allowed_write_path_prefixes were scoped to docs/dev/ and /tmp/ paths, not /root/orchestra/. Data-analysis output to /root/orchestra/docs/research/ was not anticipated as a ba-role operation at policy design time.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: pretool-tool-policy.py exits 2 (BLOCKED) before the /allow grant system has any effect. The /allow sentinel grant in pretool-bash-safety.sh only matters if pretool-tool-policy.py passes first. Because hooks run sequentially and all non-zero exits block the tool, a BLOCKED from pretool-tool-policy.py overrides any SENTINEL_OK from pretool-bash-safety.sh.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Current state; no regression event identified
observations: is_allowed('ba', 'Write', '/root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3') = (False, 'write target not in allowed_write_path_prefixes') — observed: direct Python execution (hooks/lib/policy_registry.py:260-276)
is_allowed('ba', 'Bash', None) = (True, 'ok') — observed: direct Python execution (Python executable itself not the denied object)

_looks_regex('/tmp/xlsx-venv/bin/python') = False — observed: direct Python test (no metacharacters)
userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:86-97: first bare token = PATTERN, subsequent = COMMENT — inferred: code reading, confirmed by QA verification
userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:200-205: single-part pattern → entry = {op: '/tmp/xlsx-venv/bin/python'} with NO args_contain — inferred: code reading
allowlist.py:470-472: head_op='/tmp/xlsx-venv/bin/python' == want_op='/tmp/xlsx-venv/bin/python' → True; args_contain=[] → all() over empty → True → MATCH — inferred: code reading
pretool-bash-safety.sh:483: IS_SUBAGENT != 1 guards entire sentinel block — inferred: code reading; sentinel WOULD match if reached
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: complete (primary blocker confirmed by direct Python execution; sentinel match logic confirmed by code reading; Codex confirms findings)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Codex executed python3 hooks/pretool-tool-policy.py directly with the ba subagent Bash payload. Result: exit=2, 'BLOCKED by tool-policy.v1: role=ba tool=Write target=/root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3 not in allowed_write_path_prefixes'. This is the hook that produced the observed block.
root_cause_analysis.located_source: hooks/pretool-tool-policy.py:47-92 (extract_bash_write_paths + is_allowed) — contains complaint_location_keywords via the /root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3 target path check

PRIMARY BLOCKER: fires for all tools (matcher *); extracts Bash redirect targets as Write targets; checks role-based allowed_write_path_prefixes; exits 2 if denied; bypasses main agent (role=None → exit 0)

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Prevents subagents from benefiting from legacy /allow grants
CF-1: prevents comment-only attack by suppressing legacy path when structured sentinel exists but does not match
Subagent write firewall: prevents subagents from creating /allow grants

novelty_check.rationale: Prior attempt scoped entirely to pretool-bash-safety.sh allowlist mechanism (L4 logic). This attempt identifies the primary blocker as a different hook entirely (pretool-tool-policy.py) and a different layer (L3: data — policy configuration gap in tool-policy.v1.json missing allowed prefix). The investigation used runtime execution evidence rather than inferred code reading.

context.codebase_state: modified settings.json and various docs/dev files pending commit

development_approach.strategy: Three targeted fixes: (M1) Add narrow prefix (*/docs/research/case- per Codex recommendation, or */docs/research/ for broader research output) to ba and general-purpose allowed_write_path_prefixes in policies/tool-policy.v1.json; (M2) Extend sentinel block in pretool-bash-safety.sh (remove IS_SUBAGENT guard at line 483) while keeping IS_SUBAGENT guard at line 145; (M3) Align task ID derivation: reader should use session_id as final fallback without preferring task_id field. Optional (S1): skip sentinel write for is_regex=true grants in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh.
development_approach.validation_approach: AC1: all PreToolUse hooks (bash-safety + tool-policy) exit 0 for ba subagent payload + valid sentinel after M1+M2+M3. AC4: tool-policy exits 0 for ba subagent + redirect to /root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3 after M1. AC3: pretool-bash-safety.sh reaches sentinel block for IS_SUBAGENT=1 and returns SENTINEL_OK after M2. AC5: task ID writer/reader produce same key for all four derivation scenarios after M3.

_risk_level_doc: medium: changes to security-relevant hook logic and policy config; affects allow-grant system and role-based write policy; blast radius limited to 3 files; all daemon-restart and IS_SUBAGENT legacy firewalls explicitly excluded from scope

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex session 019e4f2e-df64-7480-9845-6b965d0114cc confirmed 'root cause is basically correct'. Codex examined pretool-tool-policy.py and policies/tool-policy.v1.json at runtime and confirmed: (1) pretool-tool-policy.py extracts Bash redirect targets as Write targets and checks is_allowed; (2) ba role lacks /root/orchestra/ in allowed_write_path_prefixes; (3) IS_SUBAGENT guard makes sentinal check main-agent-only. PROPOSED_FIX: add narrow ba allowlist entry for this research artifact location, preferably */docs/research/case- or tighter. Also recommends improving block message to clarify 'Bash redirect target denied as Write target'.

findings.issue: pretool-tool-policy.py blocks ba role writing to /root/orchestra/docs/research/ because ba.allowed_write_path_prefixes does not include that prefix
findings.proposed_fix: Add narrow prefix */docs/research/case- (or tighter) to ba and general-purpose allowed_write_path_prefixes in policies/tool-policy.v1.json. Do not add broad /root/orchestra/ access.
findings.rationale: Root cause confirmed by direct Python execution and Codex runtime file examination. Incorporated as M1 (Must Have) with Codex's narrowing recommendation.

findings.issue: IS_SUBAGENT guard at pretool-bash-safety.sh:483 skips sentinel block entirely for subagents; sentinel check is main-agent-only
findings.proposed_fix: Remove or broaden IS_SUBAGENT guard at line 483 (for sentinel block only); keep IS_SUBAGENT guard at line 145 (legacy allowlist firewall)
findings.rationale: OBJ-3. No evidence the command was main-agent-issued. Incorporated as M2 (Must Have).

findings.issue: M1 prefix must be narrow — broad /root/orchestra/ access for ba is excessive
findings.proposed_fix: Use */docs/research/case- or tighter glob pattern rather than /root/orchestra/docs/research/
findings.rationale: Codex PROPOSED_FIX verbatim recommendation. Incorporated into M1 wording.

findings.issue: Block message does not clarify which part of the command is denied; could confuse future diagnostics
findings.proposed_fix: Improve pretool-tool-policy.py stderr output to say 'Bash redirect target denied as Write target' when the denial is from Bash redirect extraction
findings.rationale: Codex observation. Noted in Technical Hints for dev. Not a blocking issue; logging as out-of-scope improvement suggestion.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex findings 1+2 incorporated as M1+M2 (Must Have). Finding 3 (narrow prefix) incorporated into M1 wording. Finding 4 (block message) logged in Technical Hints. Prior spec errors (Gap C args_contain diagnosis, R4 allowlist.py) corrected based on direct Python testing before Codex consultation.
